title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
summary: PyroDash is a cost-aware framework for token-level SLM-LLM collaborative inference, reducing the costs of serving large language models at scale. The framework collaborates between small and large language models to improve reliability on difficult problems.
why_builders_care: Builders can use PyroDash to reduce the costs of serving large language models while maintaining reliability.

title: Look Less, Think Faster: Joint Token-Compute Adaptation for Multimodal LLMs
summary: This paper proposes a joint token-compute adaptation method for multimodal LLMs, reducing inference costs by adapting both the number of input visual tokens and the computation of the large language model.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this method to reduce the inference costs of multimodal LLMs.

title: KlaatAI/klaatcode
summary: Klaatcode is an open-source AI coding agent for the terminal, providing Claude Code-grade accuracy with smart model routing and support for multiple AI models.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Klaatcode to improve their coding productivity with AI assistance.

title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
summary: GPT-Red is an automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.
why_builders_care: Builders can use GPT-Red to improve the safety and robustness of their AI models.

title: Nativ: Run AI models locally on your Mac
summary: Nativ is a tool that allows users to run AI models locally on their Mac, providing a convenient way to test and deploy AI models.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Nativ to test and deploy AI models locally on their Mac.

title: SoftReason: A Fully Differentiable Neuro-Soft-Symbolic Deductive Reasoning Architecture over High-Dimensional Perceptual Data
summary: SoftReason is a neuro-soft-symbolic deductive reasoning architecture that can reason about high-dimensional perceptual data, providing a new approach to integrating symbolic and connectionist AI.
why_builders_care: Builders can use SoftReason to integrate symbolic and connectionist AI in their applications.

title: Livetennisapi/livetennisapi-mcp
summary: Livetennisapi-mcp is an MCP server that provides real-time tennis scores, odds, and model win-probability to AI assistants like Claude and Cursor.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Livetennisapi-mcp to provide real-time sports data to their AI assistants.

title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations
summary: This paper proposes a decodability supervision method for verifiable activation explanations, improving the interpretability of neural networks.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this method to improve the interpretability of their neural networks.

title: Latent-MoE
summary: Latent-MoE is an implementation of the Latent Mixture of Experts (MoE) model, providing a efficient and scalable approach to mixture of experts.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Latent-MoE to improve the efficiency and scalability of their mixture of experts models.

title: The Blessing of Dimensionality: How Near-Orthogonality in High-Dimensional Spaces Explains Temporal Portability
summary: This paper explores the concept of near-orthogonality in high-dimensional spaces and its relation to temporal portability, providing insights into the behavior of large language models.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this research to better understand the behavior of large language models and improve their temporal portability.

title: Don't Trust the Label: License Laundering in AI Supply Chains
summary: This paper investigates the issue of license laundering in AI supply chains, highlighting the need for more transparency and accountability in AI development.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this research to better understand the risks of license laundering in AI supply chains and take steps to mitigate them.
